evidence.sync: Both TSX and PHP use identical class string 'font-heading text-lg leading-[1.2] text-charcoal' on the date/time paragraph. Venue and cost lines correctly retain 'text-lg font-medium leading-[1.2] text-charcoal' — only the date/time line changed.

Change is scoped precisely to the date/time

wrapper (lines TSX:289, PHP:154). Venue line (TSX:295, PHP:163) and cost line (TSX:302, PHP:170) correctly retain font-medium — no unintended scope creep.

visualObservations.375px: Five stacked hero sections at mobile width. Bold 'TEST: Event Hero' h1 fills the viewport well. Lime-to-teal gradient backgrounds consistent across all sections. Demo container labels and descriptions readable above each section. No event-specific fields render (expected — test page is not a tribe_events post). Layout clean with no overflow or clipping.
visualObservations.768px: Tablet width. Sections taller, h1 text larger. Content area well-proportioned. All five demo container variations visible and distinct. Gradient renders smoothly. No regressions.
visualObservations.1024px: Laptop width. max-w-[700px] content column visible at left. Hero sections spacious with correct vertical padding. All five variations render cleanly.
visualObservations.1440px: Full desktop. Five tall hero sections stack vertically. Content column constrained to max-w-[700px] aligned left within container. Footer renders correctly below. All three browsers (chromium, firefox, webkit) produce consistent output with no visual discrepancies.

output.summary: All four cumulative changes verified PASS. The new change — font-heading on date/time text — is present in both TSX (line 289) and PHP (line 154) with identical class strings. Only the date/time line received this change; venue and cost lines correctly retain font-medium. No issues found.
